findings.title: .env.* variants not ignored — only bare .env is covered
findings.description: The .gitignore covers `.env` but does not cover `.env.*` variants such as `.env.local`, `.env.production`, `.env.staging`, or `.env.test`. Nuxt 4 reads `.env.local` by default and developers commonly create environment-specific overrides. The infrastructure-setup skill standard explicitly requires both `.env.*` and `!.env.example` to be present.
findings.impact: A developer creating `.env.local` or `.env.production` with real credentials will silently have those files staged and potentially committed. The gap is especially relevant for Nuxt which auto-loads `.env.local`.
findings.recommendation:
Replace the bare `.env` entry with the three-line block:
```
.env
.env.*
!.env.example
```
The `!.env.example` negation ensures the template file stays tracked even though `.env.*` would otherwise match it.

findings.title: credentials.json not ignored — only .credentials.json (dot-prefixed) variant covered
findings.description: The existing gitignore (pre-Task 1) contains `.credentials.json` (dot-prefixed, a Claude Code workspace artifact). The plain `credentials.json` pattern — which is the standard name for Google Cloud service account key files and other OAuth credential exports — is not covered. The infrastructure-setup skill standard lists `credentials.json` as a required security pattern.
findings.impact: A developer who downloads a Google Cloud service account key as `credentials.json` will not have it blocked by git. The file could be staged and committed if the developer does not notice the omission.
findings.recommendation:
Add `credentials.json` to the Secrets & credentials section of .gitignore:
```
# ─── Project: Secrets & credentials ──────────────────────────────────────────
.env
.env.*
!.env.example
*.key
secrets/
*.pem
*.p12
*.jks
credentials.json
```

findings.title: *.jar pattern too broad — may ignore future non-wrapper JARs in non-backend locations
findings.description: The Java/Gradle section uses a root-level `*.jar` glob. The exception `!backend/gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar` correctly re-includes the Gradle wrapper. However, if any JAR-based tools or libraries are ever placed outside `backend/` (e.g., `scripts/`, `infra/`), they would be silently ignored. The pattern is correct for the current monorepo shape but becomes surprising as the repo grows.
findings.impact: Low risk in current state. Could cause confusion or silent omissions if developers add JARs in non-backend directories in future tasks.
findings.recommendation: Optionally scope the jar pattern to the backend directory: `backend/**/*.jar` with the same exception `!backend/gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar`. This is a low-priority cleanup — the current pattern matches the task spec exactly and is not wrong for Epic 01 scope.
